examples.title: TCP Server Service Primitives
examples.description: Demonstrates basic transport layer service primitives for a TCP server
examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates the transport layer service primitives: LISTEN for passive open, ACCEPT for incoming connections, SEND/RECEIVE for data transfer, and CLOSE for termination. These are the fundamental services the transport layer provides to applications.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) for basic primitives, O(buffer_size) for data

examples.title: UDP Client Service Primitives
examples.description: Demonstrates connectionless transport service using UDP
examples.explanation: UDP provides connectionless service. Notice no CONNECT or LISTEN operations - data is sent directly using SENDTO. No connection state is maintained, making it stateless. The RECEIVEFROM primitive also captures the sender's address.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is message size

examples.title: Transport Layer Multiplexing Implementation
examples.description: Shows how transport layer performs multiplexing using port numbers
examples.code:
PROCEDURE Transport_Multiplex(demux_table, packet)
BEGIN
 // Extract transport header fields
 SRC_IP ← packet.header.source_ip
 DST_IP ← packet.header.dest_ip
 SRC_PORT ← packet.header.source_port
 DST_PORT ← packet.header.dest_port
 PROTOCOL ← packet.header.protocol
 
 // Create socket identifier
 SOCKET_ID ← (SRC_IP, SRC_PORT, DST_IP, DST_PORT, PROTOCOL)
 
 // Demultiplexing: Find matching socket
 IF SOCKET_ID EXISTS IN demux_table THEN
 PROCESS ← demux_table[SOCKET_ID].process
 // Deliver to correct application process
 DELIVER_TO_PROCESS(PROCESS, packet.data)
 RETURN TRUE
 ELSE
 // No matching socket - discard or send ICMP
 RETURN FALSE
 END IF
END

// Multiplexing is inverse: bundle multiple process streams
PROCEDURE Transport_Multiplex_Outgoing(process_list)
BEGIN
 FOR EACH process IN process_list
 DATA ← process.get_data()
 SOCKET ← process.socket
 PACKET ← ENCAPSULATE(DATA, SOCKET)
 SEND_TO_NETWORK(PACKET)
 END FOR
END
examples.explanation: This demonstrates the demultiplexing process using the socket 5-tuple (source IP, source port, destination IP, destination port, protocol). The transport layer uses this to deliver incoming data to the correct application process. Multiplexing outgoing data works in reverse - combining multiple application streams.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for table lookup with hash
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for demux table where n = active connections
